reason: main() dispatch case for the set-construction-iteration verb (U05, FR-2 item 8). main() is the CLI entry — never driven in-process — so bun --coverage cannot measure its switch arms; the handler it dispatches (handleSetConstructionIteration) is driven in-process by tests/integration/t250 (valid path). Dispatch-case rows are the named second-resort allowlist case in spawn-blindspot-two-step (ii); every pre-existing verb case is unmeasured the same way.
expiry: remove when main() gains an in-process argv driver

reason: Usage-error emit in handleSetConstructionIteration for the missing-argument branch (U05). error() is `never` (process.exit), so driving this arm in-process would terminate the test runner; it is exercised by the shipped-subprocess reject case in tests/integration/t250 (exit code + byte-identity asserted). Spawn-only error-exit row per spawn-blindspot-two-step (ii); the happy path is covered in-process by the same file's in-process handler drive.
expiry: remove if error() gains a test-injectable non-exiting seam

reason: Mutation-before-reject error() emit in handleSetConstructionIteration for an invalid iteration token (U05). error() is `never` (process.exit); the reject DECISION (parseConstructionIteration returning !ok) is fully covered in-process by tests/unit/t250, and the handler's reject-with-no-mutation contract is exercised by the shipped-subprocess case in tests/integration/t250 (exit code + state byte-identity). Spawn-only error-exit row per spawn-blindspot-two-step (ii).
expiry: remove if error() gains a test-injectable non-exiting seam

reason: Unknown-subcommand usage message string in main()'s default case — the two new verbs were added to the existing single-line list. A spawn-only main() message-string row (spawn-blindspot-two-step (ii)); the surrounding default branch was already unmeasured before this change.

reason: Defensive `catch {}` in findActiveStandingGrant that returns null so a verification defect can never wedge a gate (design contract E-SDG: throws collapse to per-gate approval, never fail-open at the check). No natural corpus input throws inside the scan (listIntentDirs / readAllAuditShards / StandingGrant.parse / verifyDelegatedProvenance all swallow their own IO errors), so the catch arm is unreachable by a black-box test; the happy scan and every classification branch are covered in-process by t-standing-grant.
expiry: remove if the scan grows a throw path a test can inject

Dispatch-case rows are the named second-resort allowlist case in spawn-blindspot-two-step (ii); the pre-existing prepare/check/finalize cases are unmeasured the same way.
expiry: remove when main() gains an in-process argv driver
